title: Iliad retrospective — Lachesis enrollment quest, cycle 1 (first future-module build)
quest: Lachesis: the first registered-but-unimplemented future module to traverse the Iliad lifecycle to a completed cycle-1 implementation. Commissioned by The Eye 2026-07-18.

stages_traversed.telos: docs/runs/lachesis-authorization-1/ — live 5-seat council; authz-lachesis-1; all seats approve, gate pass (ephemeral signers, real provenance).
stages_traversed.argo: docs/runs/lachesis-argo-1/ — implemented against REALITY + 5 adversarial code-review rounds; cycle-1 declared complete by The Eye with residuals as NON-CLAIMs.
stages_traversed.clotho: HELD — weaving Lachesis's own build into the knowledge graph is a follow-on (Clotho weaver run).

held_for_the_eye: enrollment-flip: move lachesis from registered-unimplemented to enrolled + change the verify-contracts expectation
durable trust-root anchoring: pin the snapshot/manifest digest in CURRENT-AUTHORITY
what_happened: The Daedalus plan converged after 10 rounds and passed a signed 5-seat council — and was STILL wrong about its own input. At Argo reconnaissance, reading the real snapshot revealed the plan mismodeled it (assumed sha256:-prefixed ids, a `kind` field, standalone node records; reality is a signed edge-stream with bare-hex ids, `edge_kind`, locator-implied nodes). The Eye ruled 'code it and watch the next round': implement against reality, let the adversary review a running artifact. Five code-review rounds followed; the first found architecture gaps, the middle found that early fixes were shallow, later rounds drove golden real-snapshot values and delegated two design decisions (relevance formula, identity scope) to the GPT seat, which ruled (3:2:1 corrected-orientation relevance, reported-not-risk-feeding; identity Ruling A = intra-snapshot bijection + explicit NON-CLAIMs). Round 5 saw codex re-raise a finding verified FALSE twice — the signal that real defects were largely exhausted. Cycle-1 declared complete with 100 assertions green over the real snapshot.
lessons: Convergence and authorization are NOT correctness. A plan can survive 10 adversarial rounds + a signed council and still not correspond to reality — because reviewers judge the plan on its own terms; only contact with ground truth tests correspondence. The gates test defensibility, not truth.
The adversarial pipeline runs at EVERY step (plan, council, code), and reviewing a RUNNING artifact against real data finds what reviewing a fiction cannot.

Shallow fixes get caught. Patching the surface of a finding (path 'binding' without containment; blast 'tested' but never actually discriminated) is detected by the next round. The fix that bites is golden values from real data.

The adversary is not infallible either. codex re-raised a false HEX64/terminator finding; verifying it empirically (rather than implementing on its say-so) is the same discipline applied to the reviewer.
Delegating genuine DESIGN decisions to the GPT seat (The Eye's routing) resolves contested calls without the builder guessing — implement the ruling verbatim.
Honest limits beat faked completeness: irreducible gaps (deriveNodeId re-derivation, a complete import lexer) are recorded as NON-CLAIMs, not simulated.

artifacts.package: lachesis/ (ingest.mjs, measure.mjs, config/, memory/, scripts/) — zero-dependency, 100 assertions
artifacts.memory: lachesis/memory/ (IDENTITY, INVARIANTS, CONTRACTS/metrics.json [NORMATIVE], NON-CLAIMS, DECISIONS, FAILURE-MODES, EVIDENCE, comprehension-queries)
